div.h1: The Corruption: Concurrent Access Without vs. With a Mutex

g.text: Key Rules: When to Use What
f_pos after read | loff_t | 100 (A used stale len)
0x00 | atomic_long_t | owner // task_struct* of holder, or 0
0x10 | list_head | wait_list // FIFO queue of waiters
simple counter | N/A | atomic_t | atomic_inc() / atomic_dec()
bitmask flag | N/A | atomic bit | set_bit() / test_and_set_bit()
Critical: Never mutex_lock() in Interrupt Context
Your handler returns -ERESTARTSYS to syscall layer
Kernel checks SA_RESTART flag on signal handler
NEVER return 0 or positive bytes on -EINTR: corrupts stream
③ Process B: write() runs, updates state
CPU 0 | resumes with reg0=100 | sees OLD data_len
⑤ Process A: copy_to_user using stale reg0=100
CPU 0 | to_copy = min(count,100) | WRONG: real len=200
CPU 0 | cmpxchg(&mutex, 0, A) | SUCCESS — lock acquired
② Process A owns lock — reads safely
③ Process B: BLOCKED on mutex (not running)
CPU 0 | suspended (mutex held) | B cannot proceed
CPU 0 | wake_up(write_queue) | signal B to proceed
⑤ Process A: mutex_unlock() — B wakes
CPU 1 | cmpxchg succeeds | B acquires lock
CPU 1 | buffer_data_len = 0 | safe to write now

text.tspan: /* cmpxchg: if owner==0, set owner=current, return 0 */
/* cmpxchg: if owner==0, set owner=current, return 0 */

g.text: schedule(); /* yield CPU — waiter sleeps here */
mutex_lock() may call schedule() — ILLEGAL in IRQ context
Symptom: BUG: sleeping function called from invalid context
Your char device handlers: process context — mutex IS correct
buffer_data_len = 200 but reader copied only 100 bytes
Remaining 100 bytes silently lost — no error returned
Next reader: may read stale/garbage bytes [100..200]
If writer also updates kernel_buffer ptr: USE-AFTER-FREE
CPU 0 suspended — BUT mutex still held by A
CPU 1 tries mutex_lock → sees owner=A → TASK_INTERRUPTIBLE sleep
A read exactly 100 bytes with consistent state view
B wrote 200 bytes after A fully released the buffer
Invariant maintained: data_len always matches actual bytes
No silent truncation, no use-after-free, no stale reads
